senses.gloss: to pull out, to withdraw, to draw, to remove gently

examples.text: مَنْ سَلَّ عَلَيْنَا السَّيْفَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا.
examples.english: He who draws the sword against us is none of us.

senses.gloss: to suffer tuberculosis, to be consumptive

etymology.text:
Cognate to Classical Syriac ܫܠܳܐ (šəllā, “to pull out gently”) and Hebrew שָׁלַל (šālal, “to draw out”), Ge'ez ሰሰለ (säsälä, “to withdraw”).

Borrowed from Aramaic סַלָּא / ܣܰܠܳܐ (sallā, “basket”).

See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

body.text: : (M, K:) likewise applied to a sheep or goat (
); on the authority of Lh; (M;) and to a she-camel
whose teeth have fallen out from extreme old age;
extremely aged, having no tooth remaining;
on the authority of IAar. (TA.)       -A2-  See also
